name: D-Mannose
summary: Decision-ready summary: D-Mannose is best framed for recurrent UTI prevention context; urinary tract adhesion support; safety screen: diabetes medication review; active UTI needs medical care.
description: D-mannose may reduce urinary pathogens’ ability to adhere to uroepithelium, aligning with UTI prevention use; clinical confidence varies across studies.
mechanisms: Prevents bacterial adhesion (E. coli) to urinary tract epithelium.
safetyNotes: Not a substitute for treatment of active infection; review with diabetes medication plans.

claimMap.claim: v10.0: The 2024 JAMA Internal Medicine RCT used 2 g/day for 6 months in 598 women and found no significant prevention benefit; block monetized recommendation.
claimMap.notes: v10.0: The 2024 JAMA Internal Medicine RCT used 2 g/day for 6 months in 598 women and found no significant prevention benefit; block monetized recommendation.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: 2 g/day was tested for 6 months in a large UK primary-care RCT and did not significantly reduce medically attended recurrent UTI

sources.title: v10.0: The 2024 JAMA Internal Medicine RCT used 2 g/day for 6 months in 598 women and found no significant prevention benefit; block monetized recommendation.
